title: Assignment to PowerShell automatic variable ($matches, $args, $input, $error, $foreach, $this, $_)
description: Assigning to a PowerShell automatic/built-in variable name overrides session state and may cause undefined behaviour or, in strict-mode/PSScriptAnalyzer, a parse-time warning that surfaces as a load-time failure when -ErrorAction Stop is set. Re-uses are particularly common with $matches when iterating regex hits in a loop where the same name is used as a local accumulator.
fix_pattern: Rename the local variable: prefer descriptive names like $regexMatches, $cliArgs, $inputItems, $caughtError. For regex iteration, use Matches return directly: foreach ($m in [regex]::Matches($text,$pat)) { ... }
